2606.32029.title: When LLMs Read Tables Carelessly: Measuring and Reducing Data Referencing Errors
2606.32029.paper_summary: While large language models (LLMs) perform well on table tasks, they still make data referencing errors (DREs), i.e., incorrectly citing or omitting table values, despite understanding the table structure. Beyond final-answer accuracy, DREs directly compromise the correctness and reliability of intermediate reasoning steps. Yet prior studies have only offered limited, small-scale analyses. In this work, we present the first systematic evaluation of tabular data referencing errors across different models and tasks. Our results show that DREs occur across all tested models (1.7B to 20B parameters). Furthermore, we demonstrate that incorporating data referencing as a critic significantly improves answer accuracy up to 12.0%, through critic-based filtering and rejection sampling. Finally, we trained a lightweight 4B-parameter critic model that achieves an average F1 score of 78.2% in detecting both in-distribution and out-of-distribution DREs, and effectively assists inference for larger models.
2606.32029.paper_summary_zh: 雖然大型語言模型 (LLMs) 在表格任務上表現良好，但它們仍然會出現數據引用錯誤 (DREs)，即在理解表格結構的情況下錯誤引用或省略表格值。除了最終答案的準確性外，DREs 直接損害了中間推理步驟的正確性和可靠性。然而，先前的研究僅提供了有限的小規模分析。在本研究中，我們呈現了對不同模型和任務的表格數據引用錯誤的首次系統評估。我們的結果顯示，所有測試模型 (1.7B 到 20B 參數) 都會出現 DREs。此外，我們證明了將數據引用作為評論者納入可以顯著提高答案準確性，最高達 12.0%，通過基於評論者的過濾和拒絕抽樣。最後，我們訓練了一個輕量級的 4B 參數評論者模型，該模型在檢測分佈內和分佈外的 DREs 時達到了平均 F1 分數 78.2%，並有效協助更大模型的推理。
2606.32029.author: Yuqing Yang et.al.
2606.32029.authors: Yuqing Yang, Qi Zhu, Zhen Han, Boran Han, Zhengyuan Shen, Shuai Wang, Vassilis N. Ioannidis, Huzefa Rangwala
